name: Arnold Fishkind
abstract: Arnold Fishkind, sometimes credited as Arnold Fishkin (born July 20, 1919 – September 6, 1999,) was an American jazz bassist who appeared on over 100 albums.

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: So it’s you, Tristano, Warne, and who else? Billy Bauer, Arnold Fishkin, and Jeff Morton was one of his drum students at the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Bauer
about_quotes.evidence: But a couple of dates Chubby was angling around. One of them was on 52nd Street. And I was playing like I was playing with Lennie... But he put Arnold Friskin with us, the bass player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Bauer interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I think it was Arnold Fishkin who was a bass player who, because Lennie was blind, somebody had to come and get us. Arnold came and got us and took us behind the curtain.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
